item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm giving this a +1 just for having characters with names that get away from the stereotypical Western European mold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given this as the last entry in The World Has Progressed Past The Need For Other Games In Town's entries, I was expecting this to be a big climax to the whole thing. This having much smaller stakes, focusing on the two characters made it all much better. In contrast to minmin and ARD, the last collapsible actually worked really well for me: I was expecting to open the collapsible and get more content. Perhaps something about how Zhou wasn't successful in the end, or some form of extended detail about the Hanged King. Instead, I got a short perfunctory note that accentuated the bittersweet ending. I'm not sure how well that reading will hold up outside of the context of the contest or when reading in the order of the canon, but it was personally effective for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one with caveats. Coming into this after bingeing the other OGIT entries, I was feeling burned out because the style of Hanged King story-corruption was hitting the same rust-dust-and-guts beats in each article, but still liked the matter-of-fact way the Hanged King ranted at Zhou through email. Similarly, Zhou achieving a pyrrhic victory over the King was refreshing, but the ending collapsible felt like an unnecessary — and failed — attempt at one last gut punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i mentioned in chat but want to post here for posterity, i am not sure the direction of romance here is necessarily the most compelling way?? partly because of the material you're working with - the r+j story - is already well trod ground, that one expects any retelling of it to hinge around the breaking of the pattern? instead of just following thru

I think that is what makes the final collapsible feel contrived, it works visually but not textually? like it's a lot cheaper when the reader is just TOLD the Griever is You……. vs. a visual format where context clues can lend more nuance to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good, definitely a +1 for me. That voicemail at the end was built up to really well.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is the final entry of Team "The World Has Progressed Past The Need For Other Games In Town."

I'd like to thank the members of my team, first and foremost. Riemann deserves a massive thank you for sticking with the proofreading of this one right up until the deadline, like a real MVP. Gaffsey, your insights on the broader narrative structure of the piece, constant poking, and witticisms helped get me through the last bit of editing this thing. rumetzen, you will always be our spirit animal. Writing (and complaining) with all of you has been a blast.

Secondary thanks goes out to A Random Day and Grigori Karpin for their invaluable crit, without which this would have looked very different.

Writing this thing took a few different approaches entirely, and was compounded by my IRL schedule suddenly being hit by a freight train. Still, I enjoyed writing the different narrative segments of this work a lot, and I hope you enjoy the conclusion of our story about the Hanged King.

On a different note, this is the 50th work that I've posted to this site. When I first joined here (almost) seven years ago, I was so anxious about getting a single entry up that I lost sleep about it the first time I posted something. Getting to 50 was unimaginable at the time, but I can genuinely say that I had a blast writing all of them. Thank you to everyone here who has made this place so special for me. Here's to (maybe) 50 more.
